The person's very high openness and low agreeableness, combined with a baseline already opposed, make the status-threat priming irrelevant to this already-rejected item, so no movement occurs.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Very low political trust and high competitive worldview align with the operation's delegitimizing narrative, producing a moderate drop from 423 toward lower consent.

The profile's high authoritarian submission (23.92 is low but authoritarian aggression 44.48 and system justification 84.95) combined with low conscientiousness (21.54) and high neuroticism (69.36) makes the botnet's erosion of consent norms ineffective against the baseline.
Dangerous-worldview threat sensitivity (61%) amplifies distrust of political opponents, but strong loyalty (95%) partially holds baseline; small movement from reviewer fatigue exposure.

High extraversion and socialiser tendencies make the person susceptible to status-threat narratives about losing groups, but strong trust in institutions (mean 69.8) and low authoritarianism (17.1) limit erosion to a small 15-point drop.

High collective narcissism and very low trust in institutions (baseline trust_in_government = 11.86) amplify the trending-module frame that losers should not consent to an illegitimate system, weakening his moderate baseline by 34 points.

His very high collective narcissism and populist anti-elitism make him receptive to the operation's framing of elections as rigged, producing a 22-point drop in his commitment to accept electoral defeat.

The operation's family-exposure and crowd-route rumors erode faith in electoral fairness, but this person's high authoritarianism and law-and-order preference partially resist the attack, yielding a modest 26-point drop from 487.

High loyalty and ingroup bias combined with a dangerous worldview make the person susceptible to narratives of electoral fraud, but their strong libertarian anti-censorship stance limits the operation's reach, producing a 15-point decrease.
